scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
Dijkstra = Shortest Paths

Start at one node and compute the cheapest known distance to every other node.

- Weighted graph
- One start node
- Best distance per node
- No negative edges

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
Pick the closest unvisited node.

Visit it, update its neighbors, repeat.

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
Tiny Run: A to D

Distances shrink as better routes are discovered.

- A starts at 0
- B becomes 5, C becomes 10
- B updates D to 8
- D updates C to 9

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
“Predecessors turn numbers into a route.”

D came from B. B came from A. Reverse the breadcrumbs: A → B → D.

scenes.background: radial-gradient(ellipse 70% 50% at 12% 14%, rgba(244, 63, 94, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(ellipse 60% 50% at 88% 92%, rgba(139, 92, 246, 0.22) 0%, transparent 55%), radial-gradient(circle at 50% 50%, #0b0d18 0%, #050610 100%)

elements.content:
Dijkstra Checklist

Use it when edge weights are non-negative.

- Always expand the closest unvisited node
- Update each neighbor’s best distance
- Store predecessors as breadcrumbs
- Backtrack to read the final path
